scope_basis: Primary Hanwoo myosatellite cells receive a dose-defined drone-pupae extract during proliferation.
transfer_limit: Growth medium contains 20% FBS, collagen coating, bFGF, and an incompletely defined animal-derived extract.

scope_basis: The functional models are bovine embryonic stem-cell aggregates and immortalized bovine mesenchymal stem cells.
transfer_limit: FGF2 production may be useful later, but there is no direct satellite-cell/myoblast experiment for the first-round corpus.

scope_basis: The experimental models are bovine embryonic stem-cell aggregates and adipose-derived bovine mesenchymal stem cells.
transfer_limit: The suspension and mesoderm-differentiation system is outside the fixed muscle satellite-cell expansion target.

scope_basis: Primary Yanbian-cattle skeletal muscle satellite cells receive dose-defined L-ascorbic acid during proliferation.
transfer_limit: Experiments use 10% FBS, and the highest tested dose reduces viability at the longest timepoint.

scope_basis: Primary Hanwoo muscle satellite cells receive factorial glucose and lysine levels during proliferation.
transfer_limit: Experiments use 10% FBS; PLAG1 genotype is a biological context and gene-expression effects must not substitute for cell-count effects.

scope_basis: Primary Hanwoo muscle satellite cells receive dose-defined E. cava crude polysaccharide fractions during proliferation.
transfer_limit: Experiments use 20% FBS and bFGF; extract composition varies by fraction and high concentrations can be cytotoxic.

scope_basis: Primary bovine myogenic cells are tested in conditioned serum-free medium and with identified nutrient supplements during adhesion and proliferation.
transfer_limit: Conditioned medium is produced by human carcinoma and mouse fibroblast cell lines; uncoated-surface effects, composition, safety, and weak adhesion limit transfer.
